section_miscategorizations.text: His cholesterol was 265 mg/dL and is now 145 mg/dL.
section_miscategorizations.reason: The phrasing emphasizes longitudinal change and therapeutic response, functioning as part of the clinical assessment of treatment effect rather than a simple objective data point.

section_miscategorizations.text: His cholesterol and triglycerides have significantly improved since starting testosterone therapy.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is a factual report of lab trends temporally associated with a therapy, without explicit interpretive judgment; it reads more like an objective summary of results than a diagnostic impression.

section_miscategorizations.text: The doctor will get the prescriptions.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is phrased as a narrative description of what the clinician did or will do, not a specific management order or plan item; as written it reflects reported process rather than a discrete actionable plan.

This statement is framed as initiation of a therapy that, per the assessment, has already been started and associated with improved labs; as written it conflicts with the assessment and would more appropriately be clarified in the assessment as existing therapy rather than a new plan order.
